headline: Israel‑Lebanon Front Escalates as US Strikes Iranian Drones, UAE Funds Tehran
subheadline: Israeli jets destroyed five Hezbollah launchers and a command centre in southern Lebanon while red‑alert incidents hit four northern Israeli towns.
lede: Red‑alert incidents were logged at Betzet, Manara, Margaliot and Metulla in northern Israel as tensions rise on the border. The Israeli Air Force struck southern Lebanon, destroying five Hezbollah rocket launchers and a command centre, and the IDF reported roughly 310 attacks in Lebanon this week. Meanwhile the United States intercepted Iranian one‑way drones near the Strait of Hormuz and the UAE confirmed the transfer of over $3 billion of a $10 billion package to Iran.

sections.title: Russia Expands Drone Launch Capacity Near Belarus
sections.body: Russia has opened five new drone launch complexes near the Belarusian border in the Bryansk, Oryol and Smolensk regions, complicating Ukrainian air defence. Ukrainian air command warned of a high probability of Oreshnik missile launches from Kapustin Yar within the next day. A separate alert warned of a medium‑range ballistic missile from the same site, while UAV groups moved toward Kryvyi Rih from the south and Pavlohrad from the east.

sections.title: Israel‑Lebanon Clashes Intensify Amid US‑Iran Skirmish
sections.body: Red‑alert incidents were recorded at Betzet, Manara, Margaliot and Metulla, prompting heightened security along Israel’s northern frontier. The Israeli Air Force struck southern Lebanon, destroying five Hezbollah rocket launchers and a command centre, while the IDF logged about 310 attacks in Lebanon over the past week. U.S. missiles engaged Iranian one‑way drones heading to the Strait of Hormuz, with explosions heard near Qeshm Island and Sirik County, and the UAE announced the release of $10 billion to Iran, over $3 billion already transferred.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The convergence of multiple red‑alert incidents and intensive Israeli strikes signals a rapid escalation on the Israel‑Lebanon front. Simultaneous US missile interceptions of Iranian drones and substantial UAE financial transfers could embolden Tehran, expanding the regional conflict scope.
